title: Nova Lingua Body Expression
description: Structured AST for the executable body of a Nova Lingua function. Function records reference a body by `body_hash` (a content-address with the `expr_` prefix); this schema defines the body that hash resolves to. Covers nine expression kinds — `var`, `lit`, `app`, `let`, `lambda`, `case`, `field`, `variant` (construction, with a computed payload), `tuple` (construction, with computed elements) — and five pattern kinds — `wildcard`, `bind`, `variant`, `lit`, `tuple`. Conditional `if` is intentionally absent: write it as `case` on a `bool` value (principle 7: minimal orthogonal vocabulary). Embedded types (in `lambda.params[].type`) and embedded values (in `lit.value` and `pat_lit.value`) are accepted as opaque objects at this layer; they must validate independently against type-expression.schema.json and value-expression.schema.json respectively. v0.2+ may tighten this via cross-file $refs once schema-resolution tooling exists. Well-formedness rules NOT enforced here (left to the verifier): variable scoping (every `var.name` bound by an enclosing function input, `let`, `lambda` param, or `case` pattern binding), pattern exhaustiveness in `case`, single-name-binding-per-arm, type-correctness of `app.fn` against args, payload-pattern-only-where-the-variant-carries-one. The body is just an expression — the function's parameter binding is on the OUTSIDE (typically the body is itself a `lambda` whose params match the function's signature). For deferred-to-later versions of this schema: type annotations on let bindings (currently inferred), multi-arm lambda equivalence, multi-binding let (currently chained), do-notation / monadic sugar, and (much later) effect rows / handler expressions.

elems.description: The element EXPRESSIONS, in order — tuple construction with computed elements (`(f a, g b)`). At least two (a 1-tuple is the element itself; the empty tuple is `unit`). The expression-level counterpart of the `tuple` VALUE kind. Destructured by a `tuple` pattern in a `case`, or the 2-tuple `fst`/`snd` builtins.

tuple_expr.description: Construct a tuple. The heterogeneous positional product; its type is the `tuple` type-expression kind, its value the `tuple` value kind. Added when in-language tuples were pulled (dependent multi-accumulator folds and tuple-returning functions).

tag.description: Variant tag, PascalCase per the sum-type convention.

payload.description: The payload EXPRESSION — variant construction with a computed payload (`Just(a / b)`). Omit for nullary constructors (`None`). This is the expression-level counterpart of the `variant` VALUE kind (which carries a value, not an expression).

variant_expr.description: Construct an inhabitant of a sum type. In the language since Maybe/Result landed; this schema entry closes a drift the commons ingest gate surfaced (bodies constructing variants were evaluator/typechecker-legal but schema-invalid).

name.description: Name in scope. Bindings come from: enclosing lambda parameters, enclosing `let`, `case` pattern bindings, the enclosing function's own inputs, or a name from the outer environment (e.g. a built-in or a content-addressed function reference declared as a name).

value.description: A Nova Lingua value. Must validate against value-expression.schema.json; checked structurally only here.

fn.description: The function being applied. Typically a `var` (resolving to a built-in or named binding), a `lit` containing a `fn_ref` value, or another `app` (curried/higher-order). Could also be a `lambda` for an immediately-invoked anonymous function.

args.description: Positional arguments. Uncurried; arity-checking is the verifier's job.

let.description: Single-binding `let name = value in body`. Multiple bindings are written as a chain. Type of the bound name is inferred in v0.1; v0.2+ may add an optional explicit type annotation.

type.description: Parameter type. OPTIONAL in v0.1: omit it to let the type be inferred (consistent with let-binding inference), or supply it when an explicit annotation aids verification. When present it must validate against type-expression.schema.json; checked structurally only here.

params.description: Lambda parameters in positional order. JSON Schema cannot enforce 'unique by .name'; the verifier checks. Zero-arg lambdas (empty params) ARE permitted — they're sometimes useful for deferring a computation.

arms.description: Match arms in order. The verifier checks pattern exhaustiveness against the scrutinee's type.

name.description: Field name to project out. Verifier checks the record type actually has this field.

pattern.description: A pattern in a `case` arm. Discriminated by `kind`.

elems.description: Sub-patterns matched positionally against the tuple's elements. Arity must equal the tuple's; each element gets its own pattern. Bindings across elements must be disjoint (single-name-binding-per-arm, enforced by the verifier).

pat_tuple.description: Destructure a tuple positionally — `(x, y)` in a `case` (the reader side of tuple construction; the usual way to read a multi-value result). The product counterpart of `pat_variant`'s sum.

pat_bind.description: Match anything and bind the matched value to `name` for use in the arm's body.

tag.description: Variant tag, PascalCase per the sum-type convention. Verifier checks against the scrutinee's sum type.

payload.description: Pattern matched against the variant's payload. Omit for nullary variants. Required when the variant carries a payload.

pat_variant.description: Match a specific sum variant by its tag, optionally destructuring its payload with an inner pattern.

value.description: Match against this literal value. Must validate against value-expression.schema.json; checked structurally only here.

pat_lit.description: Match a specific literal (e.g., a particular int, the bool true, or the variant tag for an enum-like sum). Note: `kind: "lit"` here is the *pattern* kind named `lit`; not to be confused with the body-expression `lit` (they share a kind name because they share semantics — a literal value — but live in different oneOf groups).
